message:
fix(messaging/redis): cancel-safe PubSub via PubSubControlGuard and connection poisoning

Redis PubSub control operations (SUBSCRIBE, PSUBSCRIBE, UNSUBSCRIBE,
PUNSUBSCRIBE, PING) are multi-step protocol exchanges: the client sends
a command and then reads one or more ack/event responses. If the future
is dropped mid-exchange (timeout, select!, task cancellation), the
connection is left in an indeterminate protocol state — the server may
have processed the command and sent a response that the client never
reads, desynchronizing all subsequent operations.

Introduce PubSubControlGuard — an RAII wrapper that:
1. Snapshots channels/patterns before the exchange begins
2. On successful completion (commit()), does nothing — state is valid
3. On drop without commit (cancellation/error):
   - Restores the channel/pattern lists to their pre-exchange snapshot
   - Clears pending events that may contain partial ack sequences
   - Sets a poisoned flag on the connection
   - Calls stream.shutdown(Both) so the peer sees EOF immediately

All control methods (subscribe, psubscribe, unsubscribe, punsubscribe,
ping) now use the guard. next_event() checks the poisoned flag and
rejects reads with a clear error directing the caller to reconnect.

The reconnect() method clears the poisoned flag after establishing a
fresh connection and re-subscribing to previously confirmed channels.
Also fixes a bug in reconnect() where channels/patterns were cleared
instead of being restored from the saved snapshot.

Adds regression test:
- pubsub_cancelled_subscribe_poison_connection_and_requires_reconnect:
  drives a SUBSCRIBE until the server observes the command, drops the
  future, then verifies channels are empty (snapshot restored), further
  operations fail with the poisoned error, and the connection is closed

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
